questions.text: Have interpretable-by-design models (e.g., rule-based systems or decision trees) been prioritized over black-box systems for high-stakes decisions?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Have oversight functions such as monitoring, alarms, and intervention capabilities been integrated into the system architecture?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Can the system evaluate whether the conditions needed to reliably complete a task are met before proceeding?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Are guardrails implemented to halt or modify the system's actions when it operates under conditions for which it is ill-equipped, and are such situations clearly communicated to users?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Has the design considered risks to health, safety, or fundamental rights that may emerge during intended or foreseeable misuse conditions?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Has the system avoided prioritizing automation over manual input to ensure operators can act swiftly when intervention is needed?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: What is the defined level of autonomy of the AI system?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Is this level of autonomy appropriate for the context in which the AI system will be used?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Have natural persons assigned human oversight roles been informed of the system's capacities, and through which mechanisms?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Have natural persons assigned human oversight roles been informed of the system's limitations, and through which mechanisms?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Have regular external audits been planned or implemented to evaluate both technical performance and human oversight effectiveness?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Is the allocation of roles and responsibilities between human operators and the AI system clearly defined?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Has a data retention period for oversight logs been defined that aligns with regulatory and risk requirements?
questions.category: Human oversight measures for risk mitigation

questions.text: Are alarms and alert mechanisms implemented to detect anomalies, safety risks, or unexpected outputs?
Have alarm thresholds and prioritization mechanisms been tested and validated for accuracy and reliability?
Are oversight tools accessible and usable by designated personnel during critical system operations?
Are all human interventions, overrides, and disregard actions logged with timestamps and operator identity?
Do logs capture inputs, outputs, and context surrounding each intervention or anomaly detection?
Are oversight logs integrated with the broader logging and risk-management framework?
Are access controls applied to oversight logs specifying who can view, modify, or delete them?
Does the logging process achieve adequate coverage of relevant system events?
Do logs include complete metadata such as timestamps, inputs, and outputs?
Are data retention and retrieval procedures compliant with retention rules and performance requirements?
Are logged events auditable, consistent, reproducible, and verifiable?
Has a sampling method been established for human review when real-time oversight of all outputs is not feasible?
Is the sampling method periodically re-evaluated for fairness, accuracy, and governance quality?
Has the "two people approval" mechanism been implemented for critical decisions, and is its effectiveness monitored?
Is there a mechanism to measure the contestability rate (i.e., percentage of AI decisions reviewed or contested by humans)?
Is override latency measured — the time it takes for a human operator to intervene once an AI output is flagged as problematic?
Is there a system in place for oversight records and logs to be made available to competent authorities upon request for conformity assessments or audits?
Are intervention functions such as pause, override, disregard, or reversal of system decisions implemented and tested?
Do oversight interfaces allow manual control and corrective action without causing cascading failures?
Have stress or degraded-condition tests confirmed that human intervention remains possible under adverse conditions?
Is a fallback interface available and functional under degraded or constrained operating conditions (e.g., low power or limited display)?
Does the interface provide real-time visibility of system status, alarms, and intervention options (e.g., override, stop, reset)?
Does the interface provide clear visibility of the system's operational state and decision rationale?
Have usability tests with representative users measured task-success rate, time-to-intervention, misinterpretation rate, and cognitive workload?
Have thresholds, alert refresh rates, and alarm mechanisms been validated to ensure timely perception and intervention?
Are vigilance or wake-up prompts implemented to verify sustained operator attention during continuous oversight?
Has a human-factors and usability assessment been performed?
Does the system include a clearly identifiable and functional stop button?
Have users been informed and trained on the mechanisms for overriding or stopping system outputs?
Have mechanisms been implemented to detect and mitigate automation bias in operator decisions? If so, which mechanisms?
questions.category: Mitigating automation bias and human factors

questions.text: To what extent do operators rely uncritically on system outputs (percentage of accepted outputs in final decisions)?
questions.category: Mitigating automation bias and human factors

questions.text: Is the interface design tested for the risk of over-trust or blind acceptance of automated results?
questions.category: Mitigating automation bias and human factors

questions.text: Have training programs been implemented to promote critical thinking and reduce automation bias?
questions.category: Mitigating automation bias and human factors

questions.text: Does the organisational culture encourage challenge and questioning of automated outputs?
questions.category: Mitigating automation bias and human factors

questions.text: Have developers received adequate ethical and oversight training relevant to system risk?
questions.category: Mitigating automation bias and human factors

questions.text: Have affected individuals been meaningfully involved in the design and deployment of the AI system?
questions.category: Mitigating automation bias and human factors

questions.text: Are explainable AI (XAI) techniques such as SHAP and LIME implemented for complex models?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Are feature-importance scores available to rank input features by their contribution to the model's output?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Do the explanations provide consistent instance-level interpretation of feature contributions?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Do the explanations provide understandable local approximations of model behaviour for specific predictions?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Are confidence scores provided to help operators assess the reliability of system outputs?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Does the system provide a clear rationale for each decision made?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Does the system notify users when operating under conditions for which it is ill-equipped?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Are guidelines provided to users for interpreting confidence measures and uncertainty indicators?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Are XAI tools used to clarify routine decision-making processes in an interpretable way?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Do oversight interfaces enable manual control without introducing cascading system failures?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Are designated oversight personnel provided with appropriate tools, access, and authority to intervene during critical operations?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Does the interface design ensure operators have sufficient time and information to intervene meaningfully?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Have degraded-condition or stress tests verified that human intervention remains possible in all conditions?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Do interfaces minimize alert fatigue and provide clear, intuitive feedback supporting situational awareness?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Does the interface balance information clarity with avoidance of cognitive overload for the operator?
questions.category: Interpretation of outputs and interpretation tools

questions.text: Is there a "safe state" or fallback mode that can be triggered by human intervention?
Can the system transition to a defined safe state upon human or automatic trigger?
Has the safe-state transition process been tested for stability and effectiveness?
Is the fallback interface functional and accessible during low-resource or degraded conditions?
Are all safe-state activations and transitions logged and auditable?
Are users clearly notified when the system enters a safe state or operates under degraded conditions?
Are log retention and access-control measures applied consistently to safe-state transition events?
